title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Courageous Sailing Center adapted its program during Sail Boston 250 by moving activities upriver to avoid harbor crowds.
Piers Park Sailing Center successfully hosted 300 youths for sailing instruction using 23-foot Sonars during the event.
Community Boating Inc. used the tall ships festival to spark interest in local sailing opportunities beyond the event.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts issued an air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke impacting the state through 12 a.m. Thursday.
The alert warns that PM2.5 particle levels are unhealthy for sensitive groups, including those with asthma or heart disease.
MassDEP advises residents in sensitive groups to limit outdoor activities and monitor symptoms like shortness of breath.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The historic Dutch tall ship Oosterschelde is docked at Rowes Wharf for Sail Boston 2026, offering public tours.
Oosterschelde, a restored 1918 schooner, will conduct tours in Boston and Quincy before heading to Canada, Spain, and France.
Rowes Wharf Sea Grille featured a Dutch-inspired menu to complement the tall ship's presence during Sail Boston 2026.

title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room after a 2024 performance at Outlaw Music Fest.
Dylan invited Spencer to open for him on tour in 2026, starting in Boston at Leader Bank Pavilion on July 16.
Spencer, known for her genre-blurring vocals, has worked with artists like Willie Nelson, Grace Potter, and Beyoncé.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Five New England hotel restaurants recognized among OpenTable's 2026 top 100 in America.
Contessa at The Newbury and Mooo Beacon Hill in Boston were highlighted for outstanding dining experiences.
Maine's Earth at Hidden Pond and White Barn Inn, plus Connecticut's Michael Jordan’s Steak House, also made the list.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović, New England Revolution head coach, recalls childhood World Cup memories starting with the 1982 tournament.
Mitrović discusses the impact of the 1990 World Cup on his family and career, despite Yugoslavia's loss to Argentina.
As a former assistant coach for Serbia's 2015 FIFA U20 win, Mitrović values the pride of representing his country.

title: Joe Mazzulla showcased MMA training regimen, spoke about its influence on coaching the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Joe Mazzulla uses MMA training to enhance his coaching style with the Celtics, working with trainer Eric Nicksick.
Mazzulla believes the fighter's mentality aids his mental and physical readiness, contributing to his coaching techniques.
He focuses on communication dynamics from MMA to improve player-coach interaction during basketball games.

title: Report: Wrong-way driver who killed trooper had 9 drinks before Route 1 crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Driver Hernan Ramon Marrero had a blood alcohol level of 0.192 after consuming nine drinks before the fatal crash.
Trooper Kevin Trainor, 30, died after encountering Marrero's wrong-way vehicle on Route 1, Lynnfield, on May 6.
The crash occurred after Marrero ignored 'WRONG WAY' signs, resulting in a head-on collision with Trainor's cruiser.

title: The best state to move to in 2026 is in New England — and it’s not Massachusetts
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: ConsumerAffairs ranked New Hampshire as the best state to move to in 2026, citing safety, quality of life, and no general sales tax.
Maine placed fifth in the rankings due to its low violent crime rate and high air quality, enhancing safety and quality of life.
Massachusetts ranked sixth overall, topping healthcare and education but scoring low on affordability, economic strength, and safety.

title: Acclaimed Seaport restaurant Chickadee to close after 8 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Chickadee, a James Beard-nominated restaurant in Boston's Seaport, will close after eight years on July 18.
Opened in 2018 by John DaSilva and Ted Kilpatrick, Chickadee gained fame for its Mediterranean dishes and craft cocktails.
During the pandemic, Chickadee successfully pivoted to takeout, maintaining business with innovative menu offerings.
